refinement_sections.content: We need to understand what syntactic parsing is and why it is important for analyzing English sentences. Syntactic parsing means breaking down sentences into their parts, like subjects, verbs, and objects. This helps researchers study how sentences are structured.

The parser should be able to analyze different types of sentences, mainly declarative ones, but also questions and commands. We need to make sure it can identify different parts of speech and label them correctly.

We should choose some example sentences to test our parser. For declarative sentences, we can use 'The cat sits on the mat.' For a question, we might use 'Where is the cat?' And for a command, 'Close the door.'

We need to decide how we will check if our parser is working well. This might include looking at how many sentences it gets right compared to a standard answer. We should also think about how fast it runs.
We will write a short report explaining how we built the parser, what choices we made, and any assumptions we had. This will help others understand our work.
